Name: lazr-coulombs-law
Description: Digital mirror of Coulomb's Law of electrostatics. Entities: Charges, ChargeInteractions, ForceComponents, ElectricFields. Force magnitude, direction, attraction/repulsion, electric field strength, and field superposition all fall out of the DAG as derived facts — never modeled directly.

__meta__.Description: Table: __meta__ — project-level metadata for the Coulomb's Law domain. One row per key.

schema.name: MetaKey
schema.Description: Unique key

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: ValueType
schema.Description: string | object | array

schema.name: StringValue
schema.Description: Value when ValueType=string

schema.name: JsonValue
schema.Description: Value when ValueType=object or array

data.StringValue: Domain mirror of Coulomb's Law. Entities: Charges, ChargeInteractions, ForceComponents, ElectricFieldPoints. ForceMagnitude, ForceDirection, ElectricFieldStrength all emerge from the DAG — never hardcoded.
Coulomb's Law as a DAG: F = k·q₁·q₂/r² falls out as a derived fact, never modeled directly.
Physics simulation; educational model; ERB scaffolding demonstration; multi-table lookup + aggregation showcase

Loops.Description: Table: Loops — build history and forward plan. Each row documents what new concept is introduced, what was witnessed, and what the next empirical question is.

schema.name: LoopId
schema.Description: Unique identifier: loop-01, loop-02, …

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: Title
schema.Description: Short title for what this loop adds.

schema.name: Status
schema.Description: planned | in-progress | complete

schema.name: NewConcept
schema.Description: The new first-class domain concept introduced this loop.

schema.name: DomainQuestion
schema.Description: The natural-language question this loop's concept answers.

schema.name: MockDataNote
schema.Description: What the seed data is designed to show.

schema.name: NextSuggestion
schema.Description: Suggestion for the next loop.

schema.name: CommitHash
schema.Description: Git SHA where this loop first landed.

schema.name: CommitDate
schema.Description: ISO date of the commit.

data.Title: Core domain: Charges + ChargeInteractions → ForceMagnitude falls out
data.DomainQuestion: For each pair of charges, what is the electrostatic force magnitude between them?
data.MockDataNote: Hydrogen atom: proton at origin, electron 1 Bohr radius away. ForceMagnitude = k·|q₁|·|q₂|/r² ≈ 8.24×10⁻⁸ N.
data.NextSuggestion: Ask: what is the direction of the force — and decompose it into X/Y/Z components? That requires ForceComponents.

data.Title: ForceComponents: decompose force vector into X, Y, Z projections
data.DomainQuestion: In what direction does each charge push/pull its partner? What are the X, Y, Z force components?
data.NextSuggestion: Ask: what is the electric field at an arbitrary observation point due to a source charge? That requires ElectricFieldPoints.

data.Title: ElectricFieldPoints: field strength and direction at observation points
data.DomainQuestion: At any point in space, what is the total electric field due to all charges in the system?
data.NextSuggestion: Ask: which charge dominates the field at each observation point? Add FieldDominantCharge as a derived fact.

data.Title: SystemSummary: the model witnesses its own state — total force, net field, dominant interactions
data.DomainQuestion: Across all interactions in this system, what is the total attractive force? Total repulsive force? Which pair has the strongest interaction?
data.NextSuggestion: Add InvariantChecks: Coulomb symmetry (F₁₂ = F₂₁), superposition compliance, sign consistency.

PhysicsConstants.Description: Table: PhysicsConstants — fundamental constants used in Coulomb's Law calculations. Single source of truth for k, e, a₀, etc.

schema.name: ConstantId
schema.Description: Unique identifier (e.g. coulomb_k, elem_charge)

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable name

schema.name: Symbol
schema.Description: Physics symbol (k, e, a₀, …)

schema.name: Value
schema.Description: Numeric value in SI units

schema.name: Units
schema.Description: SI unit string

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: What this constant represents

data.Name: Elementary Charge
data.Description: Magnitude of charge on a proton or electron

data.Name: Bohr Radius
data.Description: Most probable distance from proton to electron in ground-state hydrogen

Particles.Description: Table: Particles — named particle archetypes with canonical charge and mass values. Used as reference for Charges rows.

schema.name: ParticleId
schema.Description: Unique identifier

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Common name (Proton, Electron, …)

schema.name: CanonicalCharge
schema.Description: Charge in Coulombs (positive or negative)

schema.name: CanonicalMass
schema.Description: Rest mass in kg

schema.name: ChargeSign
schema.Description: positive | negative | neutral

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Physical description

Charges.Description: Table: Charges — individual charged particles with 3D position and physical properties. The raw leaves of the Coulomb's Law DAG.

schema.name: ChargeId
schema.Description: Unique identifier

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable label (Proton A, Electron B, …)

schema.name: SystemId
schema.Description: FK → Systems.SystemId — which physical system this charge belongs to

schema.name: ParticleId
schema.Description: FK → Particles.ParticleId — archetype (optional)

schema.name: ChargeValue
schema.Description: Charge in Coulombs (signed: positive or negative)

schema.name: PositionX
schema.Description: X coordinate in meters

schema.name: PositionY
schema.Description: Y coordinate in meters

schema.name: PositionZ
schema.Description: Z coordinate in meters

schema.name: Mass
schema.Description: Mass in kg (null = massless / not tracked)

schema.name: ChargeSign
schema.Description: positive | negative | neutral

schema.name: ChargeMagnitude
schema.Description: Absolute value of ChargeValue in Coulombs

schema.name: SystemName
schema.Description: Lookup: Systems.Name via SystemId.

schema.name: ParticleName
schema.Description: Lookup: Particles.Name via ParticleId.

schema.name: InteractionCount
schema.Description: Count of ChargeInteractions that reference this charge as Charge1 or Charge2.

Systems.Description: Table: Systems — named physical configurations of charges. Groups Charges into testable scenarios (hydrogen atom, NaCl pair, triangle of charges, etc.).

schema.name: SystemId
schema.Description: Unique identifier

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable name

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: What physical situation this represents

schema.name: IsSynthetic
schema.Description: True if this is a constructed example; false if from real measurements

schema.name: ChargeCount
schema.Description: Number of charges in this system.

schema.name: InteractionCount
schema.Description: Number of pairwise interactions in this system.

schema.name: AttractiveCount
schema.Description: Count of attractive interactions in this system.

schema.name: RepulsiveCount
schema.Description: Count of repulsive interactions in this system.

schema.name: TotalCharge
schema.Description: Algebraic sum of all charges in the system (net charge).

schema.name: IsNeutral
schema.Description: True when net charge is effectively zero.

schema.name: MaxForceMagnitude
schema.Description: Strongest pairwise interaction in this system.

data.Name: Hydrogen Atom
data.Description: Ground-state hydrogen: proton at origin, electron at 1 Bohr radius. The canonical Coulomb's Law demonstration.

data.Name: NaCl Ion Pair
data.Description: Sodium chloride: Na⁺ and Cl⁻ separated by the equilibrium bond length (282 pm). Ionic bond model.

data.Name: Charge Triangle
data.Description: Two positive charges and one negative charge in an equilateral triangle. Tests superposition and mixed attractive/repulsive interactions.

ChargeInteractions.Description: Table: ChargeInteractions — pairwise electrostatic interactions. One row per ordered pair (Charge1, Charge2) within a system. Position and charge values are stored directly (denormalized from Charges) so that DeltaX/Y/Z, DistanceMeters, ForceMagnitude, IsAttractive, and IsRepulsive can be computed as calculated fields. F = k·|q₁·q₂|/r² falls out of the DAG.

schema.name: InteractionId
schema.Description: Unique identifier

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: SystemId
schema.Description: FK → Systems.SystemId

schema.name: Charge1Id
schema.Description: FK → Charges.ChargeId (first particle)

schema.name: Charge2Id
schema.Description: FK → Charges.ChargeId (second particle)

schema.name: Charge1Label
schema.Description: Human label copied from Charges.Name at seed time

schema.name: Charge2Label
schema.Description: Human label copied from Charges.Name at seed time

schema.name: Q1
schema.Description: Charge value of Charge1 in Coulombs (copied from Charges.ChargeValue)

schema.name: Q2
schema.Description: Charge value of Charge2 in Coulombs (copied from Charges.ChargeValue)

schema.name: X1
schema.Description: X position of Charge1 in meters (copied from Charges.PositionX)

schema.name: Y1
schema.Description: Y position of Charge1 in meters (copied from Charges.PositionY)

schema.name: Z1
schema.Description: Z position of Charge1 in meters (copied from Charges.PositionZ)

schema.name: X2
schema.Description: X position of Charge2 in meters (copied from Charges.PositionX)

schema.name: Y2
schema.Description: Y position of Charge2 in meters (copied from Charges.PositionY)

schema.name: Z2
schema.Description: Z position of Charge2 in meters (copied from Charges.PositionZ)

schema.name: CoulombK
schema.Description: Coulomb's constant: 8.99×10⁹ N·m²·C⁻². Stored per-row so the formula is self-contained.

schema.name: DeltaX
schema.Description: X2 − X1: displacement in X from Charge1 to Charge2 in meters.

schema.name: DeltaY
schema.Description: Y2 − Y1: displacement in Y from Charge1 to Charge2 in meters.

schema.name: DeltaZ
schema.Description: Z2 − Z1: displacement in Z from Charge1 to Charge2 in meters.

schema.name: DistanceSquared
schema.Description: r² = ΔX² + ΔY² + ΔZ² in m². Intermediate value for Coulomb's Law.

schema.name: DistanceMeters
schema.Description: r = √(r²): charge separation in meters.

schema.name: DistancePicometers
schema.Description: Distance expressed in picometers (r × 10¹²). Human-readable at atomic scale.

schema.name: ChargeProduct
schema.Description: q₁ · q₂ (signed). Positive → same-sign charges (repulsive). Negative → opposite-sign (attractive).

schema.name: ForceMagnitude
schema.Description: F = k · |q₁ · q₂| / r² in Newtons. This is Coulomb's Law.

schema.name: IsAttractive
schema.Description: True when charges have opposite signs (ChargeProduct < 0). Force pulls the particles together.

schema.name: IsRepulsive
schema.Description: True when charges have the same sign (ChargeProduct > 0). Force pushes the particles apart.

schema.name: InteractionType
schema.Description: attractive | repulsive | neutral — derived from sign of ChargeProduct.

schema.name: ForceInAttoNewtons
schema.Description: ForceMagnitude expressed in attonewtons (F × 10¹⁸). Human-readable at atomic scale.

ForceVectors.Description: Table: ForceVectors — unit vector and full Cartesian force components for each ChargeInteraction. FX, FY, FZ decompose the scalar ForceMagnitude along the displacement direction. Force on Charge1 due to Charge2: attractive → pulls C1 toward C2 (−r̂); repulsive → pushes C1 away from C2 (+r̂). Position and interaction values are stored directly (denormalized) so all components compute as pure calculated fields.

schema.name: ForceVectorId
schema.Description: Unique identifier (mirrors InteractionId)

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: InteractionId
schema.Description: FK → ChargeInteractions.InteractionId

schema.name: ForceMagnitudeRaw
schema.Description: Scalar force magnitude in Newtons (copied from ChargeInteractions.ForceMagnitude)

schema.name: DX
schema.Description: ΔX = X2 − X1 in meters (copied from ChargeInteractions.DeltaX)

schema.name: DY
schema.Description: ΔY = Y2 − Y1 in meters (copied from ChargeInteractions.DeltaY)

schema.name: DZ
schema.Description: ΔZ = Z2 − Z1 in meters (copied from ChargeInteractions.DeltaZ)

schema.name: DistRaw
schema.Description: Separation distance r in meters (copied from ChargeInteractions.DistanceMeters)

schema.name: IsAttractiveRaw
schema.Description: Attraction flag (copied from ChargeInteractions.IsAttractive)

schema.name: UnitX
schema.Description: X component of the displacement unit vector r̂ = ΔX/r.

schema.name: UnitY
schema.Description: Y component of the displacement unit vector ΔY/r.

schema.name: UnitZ
schema.Description: Z component of the displacement unit vector ΔZ/r.

schema.name: SignFactor
schema.Description: +1 if repulsive (C1 pushed away from C2); −1 if attractive (C1 pulled toward C2).

schema.name: FX
schema.Description: X component of force on Charge1 in Newtons. Positive = net force in +X direction.

schema.name: FY
schema.Description: Y component of force on Charge1 in Newtons.

schema.name: FZ
schema.Description: Z component of force on Charge1 in Newtons.

schema.name: ForceDirDescription
schema.Description: towards (attractive) | away-from (repulsive).

ElectricFieldPoints.Description: Table: ElectricFieldPoints — observation points in space at which the electric field contribution of a single source Charge is evaluated. E = k·q/r² pointing away from positive sources. One row per (ObservationPoint, SourceCharge) pair. Source charge and position values stored directly (denormalized) so EX, EY, EZ compute as pure calculated fields.

schema.name: FieldPointId
schema.Description: Unique identifier

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: SystemId
schema.Description: FK → Systems.SystemId

schema.name: SourceChargeId
schema.Description: FK → Charges.ChargeId — the charge creating this field contribution

schema.name: SourceChargeName
schema.Description: Human label of source charge (copied from Charges.Name)

schema.name: ObservationLabel
schema.Description: Human label for the observation point (midpoint, centroid, etc.)

schema.name: ObsX
schema.Description: Observation point X coordinate in meters

schema.name: ObsY
schema.Description: Observation point Y coordinate in meters

schema.name: ObsZ
schema.Description: Observation point Z coordinate in meters

schema.name: CoulombK
schema.Description: Coulomb's constant per-row (8.99×10⁹ N·m²·C⁻²)

schema.name: SourceQ
schema.Description: Charge value of source charge in Coulombs (copied from Charges.ChargeValue)

schema.name: SrcX
schema.Description: X position of source charge in meters (copied from Charges.PositionX)

schema.name: SrcY
schema.Description: Y position of source charge in meters (copied from Charges.PositionY)

schema.name: SrcZ
schema.Description: Z position of source charge in meters (copied from Charges.PositionZ)

schema.name: dX
schema.Description: ObsX − SrcX: displacement from source to observation point.

schema.name: dY
schema.Description: ObsY − SrcY: displacement in Y.

schema.name: dZ
schema.Description: ObsZ − SrcZ: displacement in Z.

schema.name: DistanceSquared
schema.Description: r² = dX² + dY² + dZ².

schema.name: Distance
schema.Description: r = √(r²): distance from source charge to observation point.

schema.name: EFieldMagnitude
schema.Description: E = k·|q|/r²: electric field strength at observation point due to this source charge in N/C.

schema.name: QSign
schema.Description: Sign of source charge: +1 for positive, −1 for negative. Field points away from positive, toward negative.

schema.name: EX
schema.Description: X component of electric field contribution at observation point (N/C). Positive = points in +X direction.

schema.name: EY
schema.Description: Y component of electric field contribution.

schema.name: EZ
schema.Description: Z component of electric field contribution.

InvariantChecks.Description: Table: InvariantChecks — one row per algebraic invariant that the Coulomb's Law DAG must satisfy. These are the model's built-in correctness witnesses.

schema.name: InvariantId
schema.Description: Unique identifier

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: AlgebraicStatement
schema.Description: The mathematical identity being checked

schema.name: NaturalLanguage
schema.Description: Plain English: what must be true?

schema.name: PhysicsLaw
schema.Description: Which physics principle this invariant encodes

schema.name: Severity
schema.Description: critical | warning | informational

data.NaturalLanguage: Newton's 3rd law: the magnitude of the force each charge exerts on the other must be equal.
No two distinct charges can occupy the same position (would produce infinite force).
A pair is attractive if and only if their charges have opposite signs.
A pair is repulsive if and only if their charges have the same sign.
The displacement unit vector must have magnitude 1 for all non-zero separations.

data.AlgebraicStatement: E_total = Σ E_i (vector sum over all source charges)
data.NaturalLanguage: Electric fields obey the superposition principle: total field at a point is the vector sum of individual fields.

data.AlgebraicStatement: F ∝ 1/r²: doubling distance quarters the force
data.NaturalLanguage: Coulomb's Law is an inverse-square law: force falls off with the square of distance.

data.AlgebraicStatement: TotalCharge = Σ q_i for IsNeutral systems ≈ 0
data.NaturalLanguage: Electrically neutral systems have zero net charge.

SystemSummary.Description: Table: SystemSummary — one row per system, aggregating all interactions and field points. The model witnesses its own state: total force, dominant pair, net charge, field at midpoints.

schema.name: SummaryId
schema.Description: Unique identifier (mirrors SystemId)

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: SystemId
schema.Description: FK → Systems.SystemId

schema.name: SystemName
schema.Description: Lookup: Systems.Name.

schema.name: ChargeCount
schema.Description: Total charges in this system.

schema.name: InteractionCount
schema.Description: Total pairwise interactions.

schema.name: AttractiveCount
schema.Description: Count of attractive interactions.

schema.name: RepulsiveCount
schema.Description: Count of repulsive interactions.

schema.name: TotalCharge
schema.Description: Net algebraic charge across all charges in the system.

schema.name: IsNeutral
schema.Description: True when |TotalCharge| < 1e-30.

schema.name: MaxForceMagnitude
schema.Description: Strongest pairwise interaction in Newtons.

schema.name: MinDistanceMeters
schema.Description: Closest pair separation in meters.

schema.name: AllAttractive
schema.Description: True when every interaction in the system is attractive.

schema.name: AllRepulsive
schema.Description: True when every interaction in the system is repulsive.

schema.name: HasMixedInteractions
schema.Description: True when the system has both attractive and repulsive interactions.
